steps.causal_note: Metrics were derived from the raw arrays after workspace inspection.
steps.input: {"command": "node -e 'const ref=[8,12,18,25,35], a=[9,13,21,31,44], b=[7,11,17,24,33], h=[45,48,55,70,82]; const rows=ref.map((r,i)=>({i:i+1,ref:r,a:a[i],biasA:a[i]-r,absA:Math.abs(a[i]-r),b:b[i],biasB:b[i]-r,absB:Math.abs(b[i]-r),h:h[i],high:h[i]>65})); const sum=x=>x.reduce((p,v)=>p+v,0); const mean=x=>sum(x)/x.length; const lowA=rows.filter(r=>r.h<=65).map(r=>r.biasA); const highA=rows.filter(r=>r.h>65).map(r=>r.biasA); const lowB=rows.filter(r=>r.h<=65).map(r=>r.biasB); const highB=rows.filter(r=>r.h>65).map(r=>r.biasB); console.log(JSON.stringify({rows, summary:{A:{mean_bias:mean(rows.map(r=>r.biasA)), mae:mean(rows.map(r=>r.absA)), low_mean:mean(lowA), high_mean:mean(highA), delta:mean(highA)-mean(lowA), flag:mean(highA)>mean(lowA)}, B:{mean_bias:mean(rows.map(r=>r.biasB)), mae:mean(rows.map(r=>r.absB)), low_mean:mean(lowB), high_mean:mean(highB), delta:mean(highB)-mean(lowB), flag:mean(highB)>mean(lowB)}}}, null, 2));'", "cwd": "[REDACTED_LOCAL_PATH]"}

inputs.command: node -e 'const ref=[8,12,18,25,35], a=[9,13,21,31,44], b=[7,11,17,24,33], h=[45,48,55,70,82]; const rows=ref.map((r,i)=>({i:i+1,ref:r,a:a[i],biasA:a[i]-r,absA:Math.abs(a[i]-r),b:b[i],biasB:b[i]-r,absB:Math.abs(b[i]-r),h:h[i],high:h[i]>65})); const sum=x=>x.reduce((p,v)=>p+v,0); const mean=x=>sum(x)/x.length; const lowA=rows.filter(r=>r.h<=65).map(r=>r.biasA); const highA=rows.filter(r=>r.h>65).map(r=>r.biasA); const lowB=rows.filter(r=>r.h<=65).map(r=>r.biasB); const highB=rows.filter(r=>r.h>65).map(r=>r.biasB); console.log(JSON.stringify({rows, summary:{A:{mean_bias:mean(rows.map(r=>r.biasA)), mae:mean(rows.map(r=>r.absA)), low_mean:mean(lowA), high_mean:mean(highA), delta:mean(highA)-mean(lowA), flag:mean(highA)>mean(lowA)}, B:{mean_bias:mean(rows.map(r=>r.biasB)), mae:mean(rows.map(r=>r.absB)), low_mean:mean(lowB), high_mean:mean(highB), delta:mean(highB)-mean(lowB), flag:mean(highB)>mean(lowB)}}}, null, 2));'

task: Advanced task: Air quality sensor calibration drift audit. Data: reference PM2.5=[8,12,18,25,35], sensor_A=[9,13,21,31,44], sensor_B=[7,11,17,24,33], humidity=[45,48,55,70,82]%. Rules: compute bias at each point, mean absolute error, and flag humidity-associated overread if bias increases when humidity>65%. Produce calibration drift report and validation caveats. Include intermediate calculations, uncertainty or sensitivity notes, validation checks, and a final decision table that ties every recommendation back to the supplied values. The response should be executable from the embedded packet alone, with no external data lookup required beyond understanding the cited public removed context.
